DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT MISSION BAY BLOCK 7; CHINATOWN COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CENTER, INC. RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LP, TIMOTHY SCOTT WILSON, KARINA GUERRERO PEREZ'S DEMURRER TO UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT OF PLAINTIFFS FOR DAMAGES

Appearances: Karen Ragland of Clinton & Clinton (562.216.5000) for defendants and moving parties; Ila Rosen Janfaza of the Law Offices of Ilan N. Rosen Janfaza (310.550.6000) for plaintiffs and responding parties.

Argued and the court modifies its ruling as follows: SUSTAINED, in part, OVERRULED, in part, and MOOT, in part.

Demurrer to cause of action 1 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend facts supporting a viable cause of action. Plaintiff's sole authority, Mathias v. Accor Econ. Lodging, Inc., 347 F.3d 672, 675 (7th Cir. 2003), is non-binding, dicta because battery was not alleged, and merely opines that the case's facts were "probably" battery.

Demurrer to cause of action 3 is OVERRULED. The numerosity and severity of Plaintiff's allegations, e.g., Ds enacted a policy of not inspecting units, failed to terminate employees, permitted and tolerated a common practice and culture of extreme indifference, are sufficient to meet the 'extreme and outrageous' element of IIED.

Demurrer to causes of action 4, 5, 9, 10, and 11 are OVERRULED. First, whether the breach was de minimis is beyond the scope of a demurrer. Second, although Health and Safety Code section 17920.3 requires a public safety officer to document an infestation, this requirement is absent from Civil Code Section 1941.1(a)(6), which provides that buildings must be "kept in every part ... free from ... vermin" like spiders and mosquitos. Third, habitability is an implied term that need not be alleged verbatim or by attaching a lease. Fourth, Defendant's case law does not hold that a minor cannot recover for the Complaint's theories of liability.

Demurrer to cause of action 6 is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiff's allegations lump all Defendants' mental states and conduct together. "The concept of vicarious liability has no application to actions brought under the unfair business practices act." (People v. Toomey (1984) 157 Cal.App.3d 1, 14). "A defendant's liability must be based on his personal 'participation in the unlawful practices' and 'unbridled control' over the practices that are found to violate sections 17200 or 17500. [Citation]" (Emery v. Visa Internat. Serv. Ass'n (2002) 95 Cal. App. 4th 952, 960).

Demurrer to cause of action 7 is OVERRULED. Defendant's arguments go to materiality, which is beyond the scope of a demurrer.

Demurrer to causes of action 8 and 9 are MOOT. Dismissal on file.

Demurrer to cause of action 11 is OVERRULED. Plaintiff may allege negligence and nuisance theories on the same facts at the pleading stage. Further, the numerosity and severity of Plaintiff's allegations, at this stage, plausibly state that Plaintiffs' harm outweighs the utility of Defendants' conduct.

Demurrer to causes of action 12 and 13 are SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Plaintiffs have not pleaded harms to the community. Paragraph 179 alleges that the infestation was "substantial[]" as to "Plaintiff's comfortable enjoyment," not to the community. The Court also notes that the first sentence of Paragraph 180 appears to be missing a verb in the phrase "the ... conditions the community at large." Although the Court must accept the truths of the Complaint and make reasonable inferences in favor of Plaintiffs, it is not permitted to add words to the Complaint. Paragraph 180 then says these kinds of "infestations easily spread," not that an infestation has actually spread to the community. On agreement of counsel, plaintiff is granted 30 days leave to amend from notice of entry of order. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)
